<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by /forge:init — confidence: 98% -->
# Data Model (JSON Flat-File Store)

Forge uses a JSON flat-file store. There is no database server. All records are
individual JSON files under `.forge/store/` in the target project.

## Store Layout

```
.forge/store/
  sprints/       {SPRINT_ID}.json
  tasks/         {TASK_ID}.json
  bugs/          {BUG_ID}.json
  events/
    {SPRINT_ID}/  {TIMESTAMP}_{TASK_ID}_{ROLE}_{ACTION}.json
  COST_BASELINES.json    (created on first retrospective with token data)
```

<!-- Discovered during FORGE-S01-T06 — 2026-04-05 -->
`COST_BASELINES.json` is a project-internal aggregate file (not a validated schema
record) written by the retrospective workflow. It stores rolling median token baselines
computed across all completed sprints. It is **not** validated by `validate-store.cjs`
(no schema entry) and is not distributed with the plugin. It is safe to delete — it
will be regenerated on the next retrospective run.

## Entities

### Sprint
Defined in `forge/schemas/sprint.schema.json`

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sprintId` | string | ✓ | e.g. `FORGE-S01` |
| `title` | string | ✓ | |
| `description` | string | | |
| `status` | enum | ✓ | `planning`, `active`, `completed`, `retrospective-done`, `blocked`, `partially-completed`, `abandoned` |
| `taskIds` | string[] | ✓ | References to task records |
| `dependencies` | object | | |
| `executionMode` | enum | | `sequential`, `wave-parallel`, `full-parallel` |
| `createdAt` | date-time | ✓ | |
| `completedAt` | date-time | | |
| `humanEstimates` | object | | |

### Task
Defined in `forge/schemas/task.schema.json`

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `taskId` | string | ✓ | e.g. `FORGE-S01-T01` |
| `sprintId` | string | ✓ | FK → Sprint |
| `title` | string | ✓ | |
| `description` | string | | |
| `status` | enum | ✓ | `draft`, `planned`, `plan-approved`, `implementing`, `implemented`, `review-approved`, `approved`, `committed`, `plan-revision-required`, `code-revision-required`, `blocked`, `escalated`, `abandoned` |
| `path` | string | ✓ | Relative path of primary file |
| `estimate` | enum | | `S`, `M`, `L`, `XL` |
| `dependencies` | string[] | | Task IDs |
| `assignedModel` | string | | |
| `pipeline` | string | | Named pipeline key from config |

### Bug
Defined in `forge/schemas/bug.schema.json`

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bugId` | string | ✓ | e.g. `FORGE-BUG-01` |
| `title` | string | ✓ | |
| `severity` | enum | ✓ | `critical`, `major`, `minor` |
| `status` | enum | ✓ | `reported`, `triaged`, `in-progress`, `fixed`, `verified` |
| `path` | string | ✓ | File where bug manifests |
| `reportedAt` | date-time | ✓ | |
| `resolvedAt` | date-time | | |
| `rootCauseCategory` | enum | | `validation`, `auth`, `business-rule`, `data-integrity`, `race-condition`, `integration`, `configuration`, `regression` |
| `similarBugs` | string[] | | FK → Bug |

### Event
Defined in `forge/schemas/event.schema.json`

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `eventId` | string | ✓ | `{TIMESTAMP}_{TASK_ID}_{ROLE}_{ACTION}` |
| `sprintId` | string | ✓ | FK → Sprint |
| `role` | string | ✓ | Agent role |
| `action` | string | ✓ | Slash command invoked |
| `startTimestamp` | date-time | ✓ | |
| `endTimestamp` | date-time | ✓ | |
| `durationMinutes` | number | ✓ | |
| `taskId` | string | | FK → Task (null for sprint-level events) |
| `phase` | string | | Pipeline phase |
| `iteration` | integer | | 1-based |
| `verdict` | string | | `Approved` / `Revision Required` |

## Ephemeral Sidecar Files

<!-- Discovered during FORGE-S01-T03 — 2026-04-05 -->

Forge uses a sidecar file convention for passing out-of-band data from subagents
back to the orchestrator without polluting conversation context. Sidecar files:

- Are named with a leading underscore: `_{eventId}_usage.json`
- Live in the same directory as their associated event records:
  `.forge/store/events/{SPRINT_ID}/`
- Are ephemeral — read once by the orchestrator and immediately deleted
- Are NOT validated by `validate-store.cjs` (the leading underscore is the skip marker)

Currently defined sidecar kinds:

| Filename pattern | Written by | Read by | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `_{eventId}_usage.json` | Phase subagent (via `/cost`) | Orchestrator, after `spawn_subagent` returns | Token usage fields for the phase event record |

The same pattern was established for review verdicts (written to `PLAN_REVIEW.md` /
`CODE_REVIEW.md` by review subagents and read by the orchestrator to branch the loop),
though those use Markdown files rather than JSON sidecars.

## Validation

`forge/tools/validate-store.cjs` validates all store records against schemas.
Schemas are installed into `.forge/schemas/` per project during `forge:init`.

## Schema Migrations

`forge/migrations.json` tracks schema-affecting version changes.
`/forge:update` reads this chain and regenerates as needed.
